project.contentVersion: DSM-5-TR / ICD-11 aide-memoire dataset with Australian coding-context note
project.reviewCadence: Review after DSM/ICD updates and at least annually
project.scope: Quick-reference aide-memoire for diagnostic specifiers and clinical notes. It is not a diagnostic instrument, substitute manual, treatment guide, billing/coding authority, or Australian ICD-11 implementation guide.
project.disclaimer: Use with formal DSM-5-TR materials, current WHO ICD-11 materials, Australian ICD-10-AM/ACHI/ACS requirements where relevant, local governance, and qualified clinical judgement.

scopeWarning: This is diagnostic specifier aide-memoire content, not psychotherapy, treatment, prescribing, billing, or diagnostic-manual content.

universalSpecifiers.title: Severity (General)
universalSpecifiers.description: Mild, moderate, severe (applicable to most disorders).

universalSpecifiers.title: Course (General)
universalSpecifiers.description: In partial remission, in full remission, first episode, multiple episodes, continuous, unspecified.

universalSpecifiers.title: Panic Attack Specifier
universalSpecifiers.description: Applicable cross-diagnostically to any DSM-5-TR disorder. Document 'with unexpected' or 'expected panic attacks' if they occur in context but full Panic Disorder criteria are not met.

universalSpecifiers.title: With Anxious Distress
universalSpecifiers.description: Applicable to depressive/bipolar episodes. Mild (2 symptoms), moderate (3), moderate-severe (4-5), severe (4-5 with motor agitation).

universalSpecifiers.title: With Mixed Features
universalSpecifiers.description: Applicable to manic, hypomanic, and depressive episodes. Requires 3+ subthreshold symptoms of the opposite polarity.

universalSpecifiers.title: With Melancholic Features
universalSpecifiers.description: Applicable to depressive episodes. Loss of pleasure in all/most activities OR lack of reactivity, plus 3+: distinct quality of depressed mood, worse in morning, early morning awakening, marked psychomotor change, significant anorexia/weight loss, excessive guilt.

universalSpecifiers.title: With Atypical Features
universalSpecifiers.description: Applicable to depressive episodes. Mood reactivity plus 2+: significant weight gain/appetite increase, hypersomnia, leaden paralysis, long-standing pattern of interpersonal rejection sensitivity.

universalSpecifiers.title: With Psychotic Features
universalSpecifiers.description: Applicable to mood disorders. Mood-congruent (consistent with mood themes) or Mood-incongruent (inconsistent, carries worse prognosis).

universalSpecifiers.title: With Catatonia
universalSpecifiers.description: Requires 3+ of 12 features: stupor, catalepsy, waxy flexibility, mutism, negativism, posturing, mannerism, stereotypy, agitation, grimacing, echolalia, echopraxia. Applicable to mood, psychotic, neurodevelopmental, or medical conditions.

universalSpecifiers.title: With Peripartum Onset
universalSpecifiers.description: Onset during pregnancy or within 4 weeks of delivery (DSM-5-TR mood episodes).

universalSpecifiers.title: With Seasonal Pattern
universalSpecifiers.description: Recurrent mood episodes with regular temporal relationship to a particular time of year.

universalSpecifiers.title: With Rapid Cycling
universalSpecifiers.description: Applicable to Bipolar Disorders: At least four mood episodes in the previous 12 months.

universalSpecifiers.title: Insight Specifiers
universalSpecifiers.description: Applicable to OCD-related disorders: Good or fair insight, poor insight, absent insight/delusional beliefs.

universalSpecifiers.title: Suicidal Behaviour
universalSpecifiers.description: Cross-cutting code: Current, in early remission, in sustained remission.

universalSpecifiers.title: Nonsuicidal Self-Injury
universalSpecifiers.description: Cross-cutting code: Current, in early remission, in sustained remission.

universalSpecifiers.title: ICD-11 Symptomatic Manifestations
universalSpecifiers.description: For psychotic disorders: Positive, negative, depressive mood, manic mood, psychomotor, cognitive. Rated: not present, mild, moderate, severe.

universalSpecifiers.title: ICD-11 Prominent Personality Trait Qualifiers
universalSpecifiers.description: Negative affectivity, detachment, dissociality, disinhibition, anankastia, borderline pattern.

universalSpecifiers.title: ICD-11 Disturbances in Self-Organisation
universalSpecifiers.description: For Complex PTSD: Affective dysregulation, negative self-concept, disturbances in relationships.

disorders.name: ICD-11 Dimensional Model
disorders.icd11Context: ICD-11: Mild/Mod/Severe. Traits: Negative affectivity, Detachment, Dissociality, Disinhibition, Anankastia.

definition.meaning: ICD-11 descriptor for clinically relevant personality problems below the threshold for personality disorder.
For ICD-11 Dimensional Model, mild refers to current burden across self-functioning, interpersonal functioning, pervasiveness, distress, risk, and social or occupational impairment.
definition.clinicalNote: Mild means personality dysfunction is present but some relationships and roles remain relatively stable.

definition.meaning: For ICD-11 Dimensional Model, moderate refers to current burden across self-functioning, interpersonal functioning, pervasiveness, distress, risk, and social or occupational impairment.
definition.clinicalNote: Moderate means dysfunction is broader, more persistent, and clearly impairs relationships or roles.

definition.meaning: For ICD-11 Dimensional Model, severe refers to current burden across self-functioning, interpersonal functioning, pervasiveness, distress, risk, and social or occupational impairment.
definition.clinicalNote: Severe means pervasive dysfunction, major role impairment, severe relationship disruption, or substantial risk.
